column2: Phase 6 ships two new persona-shaped audit gates on the existing AuditGateDO substrate
column3: Phase 6 ships two new persona-shaped audit gates on the existing AuditGateDO runtime substrate — ams-output-artifact-validator (watches PR diffs for files matching governed format patterns and validates them against canonical format definitions; initial scope: journal/**/*.tsv against Dolcheo+ serialization-format) and ams-oddkit-gauntlet-runner (runs oddkit_challenge + oddkit_audit on changed canon/writings/journal markdown). Each persona has its own task-definition constraint canon doc. Reusability seam: one-line add to ALLOWED_PERSONA_URIS in worker/src/runtime/audit-gate.ts plus one new caller workflow per persona; no substrate code changes. The existing reusable workflow .github/workflows/audit-gate-runtime-probe.yml from Phase 4+5 hosts both new audits without modification.

column2: All three audit gates now speak as Oddie via klappy://canon/voice/oddie-the-river-guide
column3: All three audit gates (canon-code-sync, output-artifact, oddkit-gauntlet) now speak as Oddie per klappy://canon/voice/oddie-the-river-guide. The original ams-canon-code-auditor persona explicitly set brand_discipline: null on the framing "audit gates are internal infrastructure, not a public-facing voice." Reversed in this PR. The upstream Oddie voice canon (which postdated that persona's first version) names "audit findings" as one of Oddie's flagship surfaces — maintaining a voiceless framing would produce three audit verdicts on the same PR with two distinct voices, a coherence failure. All three personas now declare brand_discipline: klappy://canon/voice/oddie-the-river-guide. Voice content is referenced by URI, not restated locally; updates upstream propagate without canon edits in AMS.

column2: Operator caught two drifts in canon authoring in real time
column3: Operator caught two drifts in canon authoring in real time. First catch: I had inline restated Oddie's voice locally in both new persona docs (with non-canonical emoji palette I invented: 📐 🔍 🧪 🔗 🧹 📋 🎯 🪞 instead of the canonical 🦦 + river vocab 🌊 🪨 🪵 🌿 🏞️ 🌀 + functional status ✅ 🟢 ⚠️ 🔴 ⏳ 🟡). Second catch: scope of voice retrofit. Originally I was going to leave the existing canon-code-auditor persona unchanged "to keep PR scope narrow," but operator pushed: "Same voice should be applied to the original drift audit." Correct — if all-agents-use-Oddie-from-upstream is the principle, the original audit gate can't be the lone exception. Both catches turned canon-authoring quality from "ships a contradiction we'd retrofit later" to "ships coherently aligned."

column2: Voice is inherited from upstream by URI; no local copies maintained in personas or constraints
column3: Voice is inherited from upstream by URI. Persona profiles declare brand_discipline: klappy://canon/voice/oddie-the-river-guide. Persona docs include a "Voice — Inherited from Upstream" section that describes only practical wiring (how the runtime composes the system prompt, which surfaces inherit which constraints) — not the voice content itself. Constraint docs §How the Audit Runs include a "Voice and surface" paragraph that references upstream and notes which river vocabulary maps to that audit's surfaces (e.g., 🪵 driftwood for dead URIs in the gauntlet runner; 🌿 banks for the format definitions the output validator checks against). Updates to klappy://canon/voice/oddie-the-river-guide propagate to all three audit-gate personas without canon edits in AMS.

column2: Each persona has its own constraint canon doc following the family naming convention
column3: Each new persona has its own constraint canon doc (system_prompt_uri target) following the family naming convention -via-spawned-agent-session. Three audit gates now live in this family: canon-code-sync, output-artifact-format-validation, oddkit-gauntlet. Adding a fourth requires writing a new constraint canon doc + a new persona canon doc + a one-line add to ALLOWED_PERSONA_URIS + a new caller workflow. No substrate changes. This is the reusability claim made operational.

column2: Oddkit-gauntlet workflow path-filtered to governance content; output-validator fires on every PR
column3: The oddkit-gauntlet workflow filters by paths: canon/**/*.md, writings/**/*.md, journal/**/*.md. Code-only PRs don't trigger it; the canon-code-sync gate covers those. The output-artifact-validator workflow has no path filter — it always fires, since governed-format files may appear anywhere in a PR and the persona's own allow-list (in the constraint canon) bounds what it audits. If the audit's scope is empty for a given PR (no in-scope files), the verdict is PASS with a summary saying "nothing to audit."

column2: brand_discipline is a canon-and-prompt declaration; runtime substrate does not interpret it directly
column3: All three personas (existing + 2 new) now have brand_discipline pointing at the same upstream voice canon. The runtime substrate (AuditGateDO) does not interpret brand_discipline directly; it's a declaration that the persona's system prompt URI's content references the voice canon. The agent invocation has oddkit MCP wired, so the agent can oddkit_get the voice canon if mid-audit register questions arise. No code changes required for voice support — voice is a canon-and-prompt concern, not a runtime concern.

column2: Phase 6 PR forthcoming; 10 files; audit gate audits itself; post-merge produces three verdicts per qualifying PR
column3: Phase 6 PR forthcoming. 10 files: canon/personas/ams-canon-code-auditor.md (modified — voice retrofit, brand_discipline flip, supersession notes for the original null framing), canon/constraints/canon-code-sync-via-spawned-agent-session.md (modified — Voice and surface paragraph in §How the Audit Runs), canon/personas/ams-output-artifact-validator.md (new), canon/personas/ams-oddkit-gauntlet-runner.md (new), canon/constraints/output-artifact-format-validation-via-spawned-agent-session.md (new), canon/constraints/oddkit-gauntlet-via-spawned-agent-session.md (new), worker/src/runtime/audit-gate.ts (one-line: 2 URIs added to ALLOWED_PERSONA_URIS plus comment block refresh), .github/workflows/output-artifact-validation-runtime-probe.yml (new caller), .github/workflows/oddkit-gauntlet-runtime-probe.yml (new caller, path-filtered to governance content), journal/2026-05-13-phase-6-twin-validators.tsv (this DOLCHEO trail). Total canon authoring: ~10,900 words across 6 docs (4 new, 2 modified). Local typecheck clean; wrangler v4 dry-run produces 2.3 MB bundle with all bindings resolving. Audit gate (still on the Managed Agents path; runtime probe is non-gating) auditing its own PR. Post-merge: all three runtime probes fire on every subsequent qualifying PR, producing three verdict comments per PR (canon-code-sync + output-artifact + oddkit-gauntlet where applicable). Parity observation accumulates organically; cutover decisions are operator-driven per gate.
